commands.review_summary:
The PR extends Codex app-server model parsing, catalog mapping, docs, and tests so optional native/effective context metadata flows into OpenClaw `contextWindow` and `contextTokens`.

PR surface: Source +39, Tests +16, Docs +5. Total +60 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main hardcodes the Codex provider catalog window and drops app-server context metadata, while the sibling Codex companion branch exposes the exact fields this PR consumes.

target.title: fix: Codex catalog honors app-server context caps
target.body:
Related: #93764

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running the Codex harness could see OpenClaw model context display and budgeting based on stale local context metadata when Codex app-server has a smaller native effective context window.

This is the OpenClaw consumer side of the fix. It consumes Codex app-server context metadata when the app-server exposes it; older Codex app-server builds keep the existing fallback catalog behavior.

Companion Codex branch: https://github.com/hannesrudolph/codex/tree/codex-model-context-metadata

## Why This Change Was Made

OpenClaw already has separate `contextWindow` and `contextTokens` model fields, which is the right boundary for native model metadata versus effective runtime caps. This change keeps that split and maps Codex app-server `model/list` context metadata into it instead of adding a model-specific clamp or changing global provider merge semantics.

## User Impact

When Codex app-server reports native/effective context metadata, OpenClaw model discovery can show and budget against the same runtime cap Codex uses internally. For GPT-5.5-style catalogs that means OpenClaw can represent `272000` native context with `258400` effective context tokens instead of continuing to imply a stale 1M runtime budget.

## Evidence

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/provider.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/models.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Codex companion branch proof:
- `just write-app-server-schema`
- `just fmt`
- `PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH" just test -p codex-app-server-protocol -p codex-app-server model_list`

What was not tested:
- Full OpenClaw `pnpm check:changed` was not run.
- Full Codex `just test` was not run.
- Live Codex app-server `model/list` with the new upstream fields was not tested because the companion Codex change is not merged/bundled yet.
target.author: hannesrudolph
